Proposed_hypothesis: H1 Procedural efficiency improves with repeated VR exposure.
Primary_endpoint: Total completion time by chronological run
Primary_result: Supported: strong run effect and significant Run1->Run2 / Run1->Run3 contrasts.
Recommended_paper_claim: Measurable learning/familiarization effect in a randomized within-subject VR procedural task.

Proposed_hypothesis: H2 Interaction errors decrease as users stabilize motor/procedural control.
Primary_result: Partially supported: large descriptive reduction, Run1->Run3 significant, omnibus not significant.
Recommended_paper_claim: Error data are promising but should be framed as secondary pilot evidence.

Proposed_hypothesis: H3 The platform is feasible for immersive industrial training.
Primary_result: Supported: high acceptance/presence and low symptoms.
Recommended_paper_claim: Usability/comfort feasibility is one of the strongest submission claims.

Proposed_hypothesis: H4 Stress-event condition produces measurable objective burden without unacceptable discomfort.
Primary_endpoint: Stress condition time/errors and subjective stress/symptoms
Primary_result: Promising but not confirmed: objective burden is descriptive; subjective stress remains low.
Recommended_paper_claim: Treat as an exploratory manipulation requiring stronger event logs in future work.
Do not make this a central ICINCO claim; mention ceiling effects.
